subject: DevOps
title: The Wall Between Dev and Ops
description: A fast breakdown of The Wall Between Dev and Ops for builders.

hook.text: Developers wrote code and threw it over a wall. Ops caught it and tried to keep it running.

buildup.text: Dev wanted to ship fast. Ops wanted stability. Their goals clashed constantly.

discovery.text: Outages happened at deploy time. Blame flew back and forth. Nobody owned the full picture.

twist.text: The problem wasn't people. It was the org chart that split building from running.

climax.text: DevOps was born to tear down that wall. One team owns code from commit to production.

punchline.text: DevOps merges building and running. No more wall.

quiz.question: What core problem did DevOps set out to solve?
options: The separation between development and operations teams
The high cost of server hardware
